construction: unilocal: ONE shared pattern phi, two probes lock simultaneously, phi back-reacts on both (g), clock-gated synchronous update
verdict: THE BELL TRADE-OFF, MADE MECHANICAL. Two regimes, no overlap: (i) NO-SIGNALING envelope caps at S = 1.994 <= 2 (g=0 recovers the local 02 result); (ii) back-reaction CAN push S up to 2.667 (at g=0.5, > 2) — but every such row SIGNALS heavily (delta up to 0.43; every-S>2-signals: True). So the dynamical global clock with mutual back-reaction DOES create genuine nonlocality — but it is a SIGNALING channel (a communication link), not QM's no-signaling nonlocality. You can have nonlocal OR no-signaling, never both > 2. That is exactly Bell/Tsirelson playing out: a real-phase substrate with a sign-threshold readout cannot produce no-signaling Bell violation, with a static OR a dynamical shared variable. The missing primitive is not back-reaction (which only signals) — it is interfering COMPLEX amplitudes (the cos^2 projection law), i.e. the complex Intent field of Phase-1.6, not a real phase. This EXTENDS bet B1's boundary from static-lambda (02/03) to the dynamical clock (04), and names the upgrade that would be required. The model does NOT reach the observed S=2.828 without that primitive. Bucket 0 unchanged.
